title: podman remote: build with `--from oci-archive:./image.ociarchive` should copy the image to the remote
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

I left it as a bug, but this could very well be a feature request.

**Description**

Note: I would have opened this issue against buildah, since it is build related, but the relationship with `podman --remote` made me open it here instead. If this should be a `buildah` bug please transfer it to that repo.

It would be nice if someone has an environment set up to use `podman --remote` that if they provided a local path to an ociarchive to `podman build --from` that the image get properly copied/imported/used on the remote for the build.  


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. export CONTAINER_HOST=foo ; export CONTAINER_SSHKEY=bar

2. curl -O http://example.com/image.ociarchive 

3. podman build --from oci-archive:./image.ociarchive


**Describe the results you received:**

The path to the image is attempted to be found on the remote and fails:

```
2022-09-13 19:23:19,203 INFO - Running command: ['podman', 'build', '--cache-ttl=0.1s', '--tag=localhost:x86_64-c940faa', '/tmp/tmpf85qmpcc/', "--label=build.commit.id='c940faad56002dc881ee5a707de42ba4951d4ba6'", "--label=build.commit.re
f='main'", "--label=build.commit.url='https://github.com/dustymabe/dustymabe.com.git'", "--label=build.commit.author=''Dusty Mabe <dusty@dustymabe.com>'", "--label=build.commit.date='Fri Nov 5 15:19:18 2021 -0400'", "--label=build.commit
.message='Some more updates for the 2021-11-02 post''", '--from=oci-archive://f37.ociarchive']                                                                                                                                               
STEP 1/23: FROM oci-archive://f37.ociarchive                                                                                                                                                                                                 
Error: error creating build container: creating temp directory: open /f37.ociarchive: no such file or directory
```


**Describe the results you expected:**

Argument to `--from` to be copied/used on the remote.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[coreos-assembler]$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Thu Aug 11 14:42:17 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: yes perhaps it is a duplicate of #15141. IIUC making this work wouldn't be too hard (just copying over a file to the remote and making it available), correct? It's just if it's worth it or not to support it?
